entry.title: What’s New in SAP Automation Pilot Q3 2024
entry.content:
In this blog post, we’ll dive into the latest updates and features in SAP Automation Pilot, designed to further enhance your automation capabilities and streamline operational workflows. Discover how these improvements can help you optimize and streamline your tasks for greater efficiency.

Content Generation Assistant - GlobalAvailability

We’re happy to announce that our Generative AI (GenAI) capability in SAP Automation Pilot reached General Availability globally on September 20th! With the Content Generation Assistant, users can now leverage AI to generate commands and workflows using natural language prompts, simplifying the automation process and minimizing manual effort.

 

For more information on this exciting update, you can check out the full blog post here.

Scheduled Executions Generation

With the latest updates, the Content Generation Assistant now supports generation of scheduled executions in SAP Automation Pilot with just a single prompt. The assistant automatically creates executions set to run at specific times, simplifying workflows and reducing manual tasks. This feature enhances flexibility and control, making operations more streamlined and easier to manage.

Remote Work Processor - Standalone Mode

The Remote Work Processor in SAP Automation Pilot can now operate in standalone mode. This lightweight agent allows users to securely make network requests in restricted environments, like private networks or on-premise systems with limited internet access. By delegating network requests to the agent, Automation Pilot can access internal resources, enabling automation in isolated or fenced-off systems. This enhancement significantly improves the tool's flexibility, making it easier to manage and automate tasks in hybrid and complex network setups.

UI Enhancements: Improved Dialogs and Expression Suggestions

The revamped Input and Output dialogs in our UI now feature a more organized structure, making it much easier to handle large JSON arrays or objects. Enhanced formatting options improve the readability of complex data, and users can hide specific fields for a more cleaner view.

 In addition to the updated Input and Output dialogs, we’ve also enhanced the Key Management Dialogs with a simplified, dynamic view. Depending on the type of data you’re working with, the dialog expands to display relevant fields, making it more flexible and user-friendly.

 

We’re also excited to announce that the Expression suggestions are now supported across all smart view text areas, further streamlining the user experience.

Content Connectors

With Content Connectors in SAP Automation Pilot, users can now back up their content directly to GitHub, either manually or on a scheduled six-hour basis. You can configure backups by selecting specific catalogues and applying tags to filter what gets backed up. Additionally, backups can be saved as individual files for each command and input or consolidated into a single file. The intuitive interface simplifies the process, making it easy to organize and safeguard your automation content efficiently.

New SAP Alert Notification service Events for Commands and Inputs

SAP Automation Pilot can now produce events for key actions on commands and inputs including:

  • Command Created, Updated, Deleted: Notifications for any creation, modification, or deletion of commands.
  • Input Created, Updated, Deleted: Real-time alerts when inputs are added, changed, or removed.

These new event notifications provide instant visibility into important updates, helping teams maintain greater control and transparency in shared environments. Whether it’s for improved collaboration or operational oversight, these alerts make monitoring changes more seamless and efficient.

Check out how to configure SAP Automation Pilot to produce these events here. 

New command in AI Core Catalog: GenerateGpt4OmniCompletion

Introducing a new command in the AI Core Catalog: GenerateGpt4OmniCompletion in SAP Automation Pilot. This command allows you to generate completions using GPT-4o for the SAP AI Core's generative AI hub.

Additionally, both the GenerateGpt4OmniCompletion and GenerateGpt35TurboCompletion commands now support response formatting, offering a cleaner and more structured view of the data, enhancing readability and usability.

New Example Scenario: Advanced Availability Check

The Advanced Availability Check monitors service availability and reports results to Dynatrace for SLA tracking and data visualization. In this example, the HTTP Bin service is used for testing, but it can be adapted for various services based on customer needs. Availability checks can also be scheduled at regular intervals using Automation Pilot’s Scheduled Executions feature. This provides a robust mechanism for continuous service monitoring, improving operational efficiency and reliability.

Check out the example in our Github repo with samples and try it. 

How to stay tuned to recent and upcoming innovations? 

  • What’s New in SAP Automation Pilot
  • SAP Road Map Explorer for SAP Automation Pilot.
  • Release Navigator for SAP Automation Pilot easing you the way to find product release related notes, blogposts, and webpages.
  • Public GitHub Automation Pilot sample use cases;

entry.title: Proxy cannot be reached while adding subaccount in Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

Recently, I had a issue in the cloud connector version 2.17.1

I have installed the Cloud connector in my personal laptop and trying to add the BTP sub account. It was giving error "Proxy Cannot be reached".

 

After analyzing the issue, I got to know that in this version it is trying to check the proxy in the system and it is failing.

 

Solution:  Uninstall the Version 2.17.1 and install the Version 2.16.2 for window and other operating systems.

The version is available on the SAP Site: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#cloud

After installing this version, issue will solve.

 

Thanks.

Regards,

Rohit Gera

entry.title: Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Diving into Your Own System
entry.content:
Introduction

From 2402 Release, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition added a new system monitoring capability, collectively called the Technical Monitoring Cockpit for monitoring the ABAP system.  The Cockpit provides a seamless connection between your system workload and single ABAP statistics records (main records and sub-records) down to SQL statements and their prepared plan.  Now you have a good starting point to conduct performance analysis on the SAP S/4HANA system in case of high response times, especially when you are an administrator or a developer. 

As this Technical Monitoring Cockpit is not unique to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but also to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and the Business Technology Platform, the discussion here applies to them as well.

I plan to tackle this topic in a series of blogs in bite sizes so that you can easily consume them during your day-to-day work.

  • Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Clicking Through: this blog provides a guided tour of the Cockpit to readers, walkthrough (clicking through) various functions and resolve some confusions.
  • Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – The Key Concepts: this blog explains what metrics we are looking for in the Cockpit, and how they can be helpful to you, especially when something seems suspicious. 
  • Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Diving into Your Own System Workload:  this blog discusses the approach as an administrator/developer to analyze the system performance daily.
  • Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Analyzing Performance with A Customized Profile (to be published):  this blog discusses how to take the advantage of setting up a profile to record system data you are interested, hence analyze the data when issues surface.

In my previous two blogs of this Technical Monitoring Cockpit series, I explained how to click through these apps and the key concepts, now let’s put them into use in your own system: how to do a system analysis as an administrator/developer daily.

 

System Workload

When talking about the system monitoring, the first entry is the System Workload app (see my previous blog on how to access it).

The below screen System Workload: Response Time Contribution gives a glimpse on how the system is utilized in the past 24 hours (Note: my most business users are in Asia, so the heavy workload appears in my midnight to early morning since I am located in US East Coast.)

  • During the past 24 hours (12 pm on Oct. 9 to 12 pm on Oct. 10), heavy workload happened around 2 am to 6 am.
  • The peak Response Time Contribution reached 360 seconds.
  • If I am curious on the peak Response Time Contribution, I can move the Time Slider to left. Comparing with two graphs of Oct. 8 vs. 9, we found
    • The peak Response Time Contribution is about the same, 300+ seconds, although they occurred at a different time slot.
    • When focusing on the highest Response Time Contribution, the pattern is similar with three high components: the Roll Wait Time, the ABAP Processing Time and the HANA Execution Time.

The Peak Response Time Contribution from 12 pm to 12 pm on Oct. 9

 

The Peak Response Time Contribution from 12 pm to 12 pm on Oct. 8

 

Now let’s narrow the time range to 2:30 – 3:15 am. I want to find out the reason of high Roll Wait Time, ABAP Processing Time and HANA Execution Time.  By clicking on Roll Wait Time in the Legend, we can see the Remote Function Call (RFC) is the main contributor, almost 93% ((56.36+56.24+49.39+8.99+8.97+8.27)/201.76). The top three programs are QDEST_RUN, QDEST_RUN_DESTINATION and ARFC_RUN_NOWAIT.

Explore the Cause of Long Roll Wait Time

 

The Roll Wait Time represents a time for which the roll context was rolled out. In the below figure, it is a major contributor to an RFC Process or waiting time until the results are returned from an external system.  This matches with the nature of an RFC call, i.e., you have no control over an external system on how fast it returns the result to you.

Contributors to the Server Response Time in a Synchronous RFC Processing (from SAP Help)

 

Now I click on the ABAP Processing Time in the Legend, the top ten contributors are listed in the table.  The top one is to run a program called /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/DataSources in an OData V2 type request.

Explore the Cause of Long ABAP Processing Time

 

Go to the details (right most > button of the record), we can see this program was run seven times as listed in the table.  The processing time is quite even, between 1.46 to 2.04 seconds.

ABAP Processing Time of Running Program /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/DataSources (At 10-Minute Intervals)

 

In the blog Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Key Concepts, I explained how the ABAP Statistics Data are collected: one minute at a time on those completed work processes (look back), and data are aggregated. How about we look at the data in a different granularity?  For example, select As Collected? That is a different view (see below).  However, we still cannot pin down which vertical purple bar belongs to which of these seven runs.

ABAP Processing Time of Running Program /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/DataSources (As Collected)

 

To get the details of each run, I click on the Details button at the right of the top row. This brings me to the ABAP Statistics Record screen with more information about this record.

In the tab Server Response Time Contribution, it provides all the metric data of this program. The large portion of the time is spent on the ABAP Processing and HANA Execution.

ABAP Statistics Record – Server Response Time Contribution

 

When I see the Database Network Time is 148.54 milliseconds, it tells me there are many trips to the database.  I want to explore further by clicking on the tab Data Connections. There are 26,326 database calls.  This confirms a lot of trips were made to the database.

ABAP Statistics Record – Database Connections

 

By clicking on the Details button again, we can see the Database Connection Details screen.  Although there are 26,326 logical database requests, only 333 physical database requests happened. From database point of view, it is efficient as most data are cached.

Database Connection Details

 

You can also explore the SQL statements associated with this long ABAP Processing Time by clicking on the tab SQL Statements.  When clicking on the first row of the program, you can see the details of this SQL statement.

SQL Statement Details

 

Sampled Work Process Data

In the blog Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Key Concepts, I discussed there are two perspectives of exploring system performance.  One is the System Workload, which has data at every minute on those completed work processes.  Another one is called Sampled Work Process Data.  It is looking at data at every second including those currently running work processes. I use this perspective to identify the running programs after I notice a peak resource consumption from System Workload.  Let me explain that.

The below figure displays the Sampled Work Process Data at the same time range as above, from 12 pm on Oct. 8 to 12 pm on Oct. 9.

Sampled Work Process Data (from 12 pm on Oct. 8 to 12 pm on Oct. 9)

 

It is tricky to isolate these colorful bars around 3 am.  If you try to narrow down to 2:30 – 3:15 am, you don’t see any data.  So, you have to enlarge the time range, for example 18:15 – 8:37. After applying filter of OData V2, I got the below chart and table.

Sampled Work Process Data: ABAP CPU Time (18:15 – 8:37)

 

In the previous section, I was investigating a high ABAP CPU Time with the following information:

  • Request Entry Type: OData V2
  • Request Entry Name: /SAP/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/0001
  • Program Context: /sap/opu/odata/sap/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/DataSources
  • Start Time: 2024-10-09 02:34:57.92458200

 

Now I click on the first row with the same -  Request Entry Name: /SAP/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/0001.  A new screen opens with a time series of this Program Context but different Program Name. When I focus on the time range of 2:34:57.92458200, there are three programs running in that vicinity, namely:

  • /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP
  • CL_ACMRT_KERNEL_PROXY=========CP
  • /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP

Request Entry Point: OData V2 and /SAP/ESH_SEARCH_SRV/0001

 

Take the last program /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP as an example. I click on the Details button on the right. It reveals the Single Work Process Sample screen. I can review the Response Time and other information.

Single Work Process Sample – Work Process Metrics

 

When clicking the “ABAP Statistics Record” button, the ABAP Statistics for this Request Entry Point becomes available. Please keep in mind that this Record is an aggregated information of this Request Entry Point in the past minute, no longer at that second. But it is still good enough for you to isolate the program you are interested in.

ABAP Statistics Record for Program /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP

 

You can do the same for other two programs: /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_HELPER========CP and CL_ACMRT_KERNEL_PROXY=========CP.

 

Conclusion

This blog discusses a daily routine work for an administrator or a developer on how to discover a high resource consumption activity, and find out the program causes that by using both the System Workload and Sampled Work Process apps.

 

References

  • SAP Help: Monitoring the System Workload
  • Blog: Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Clicking Through
  • Blog: Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – The Key Concepts 
  • Blog: Technical Monitoring Cockpit Introduction Series – Diving into Your Own System Workload
  • Blog: Analyzing performance degradations in the ABAP environment in the Cloud

entry.title: Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build
entry.content:
Table of Content

  • Motivation for Guided Experiences

  • New Guided Process Modelling: Advanced Automation and Flexibility

  • Connecting Guided Processes as Wizard Templates in SAP Build Work Zone

  • Conclusion – SAP Build Guided Experiences

  • What's Next?

  • Additional Read

Motivation for Guided Experiences

Guided experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Each step can be enriched with various types of content, including videos, documents, forms, tasks, UI cards, and apps to provide users with all the necessary information to complete each step. Steps can be classified as optional or mandatory, ensuring critical tasks are completed while allowing flexibility for other actions. Users don't need to worry about finishing all the steps at once as the progress is saved, enabling them to resume from where they left off. Guided experiences can be used for various complex processes such as parental leave, benefits enrolment, employee relocation, new hires onboarding or suppliers onboarding, training and enablement, and browsing and purchasing electronic equipment. To learn more about the background & motivation around guided experiences, please see our previous blog about this capability.

New Guided Process Modelling: Advanced Automation and Flexibility

The major enhancement in the October 2024 release is the ability to model guided processes directly in SAP Build Process Automation, bringing powerful process automation to your SAP Build projects. The following key aspects have been added:

Guided Process Artefact

The new ‘Guided Process’ artefact allows you to design and manage structured, multi-step processes within SAP Build Process Automation. This feature is perfect for creating more dynamic and reusable workflows.

Stages and Steps 

Following the structure introduced for the wizard layout, ‘Guided Processes’ equally allow to create stages & steps within each stage.  Additionally, these process steps can be designed to be sequential or parallel flows, allowing for both straightforward and complex workflows.

Create a StageAdd a Step to a Stage

 

Note: with this release, only SAP Build Work Zone is supported as ‘Step UI’; meaning the step itself needs to designed via the Work Zone wizard layout editor (for example to add UI integration cards).

Advanced Logic Integration

To create more sophisticated processes as basis for your guided experience, branches can be used to structure the overall guided process and duration checks can be incorporated via wait for duration to handle conditional starting of stages. Furthermore, subprocesses can be used to handle more complex requirements, such as retrieving hire date information.

Easy Deployment to SAP Build Work Zone

Once the guided process is modelled, it can be deployed to SAP Build Work Zone as a wizard layout template, ensuring easy availability for end-users. This provides a seamless transition from process design to execution.

Note: the deployment to SAP Build Work Zone is performed on behalf of the logged-in user via the automatically created destination pointing to DWS as per the following guide.

Shared Environments in SAP Build

This new ‘Guided Process’ artefact is only supported in shared environments, not in the default / public one. To learn more about shared environments in SAP Build, please refer to the following blog post.

Connecting Guided Processes as Wizard Templates in SAP Build Work Zone

In addition to modelling ‘wizard layouts’ directly in SAP Build Work Zone (which is now available via the “New Wizard” option)...

Add Wizard LayoutSelect Process for WizardOption for Standalone Wizard

 

… it is now possible to select ‘Guided Processes’ modelled in SAP Build Process Automation as the underlying structure when creating guided experiences.

Guided Process-based Wizards

Users can now select one of previously deployed guided processes they have access to. These serve as the template the wizard layout within SAP Build Work Zone.

Note: While modelling & deploying guided processes via the SAP Build Lobby requires access to the ‘advanced user’ license in SAP Build Process Automation, that is not the case for consuming deployed processes. For doing so, the user requires the following permissions:

  • Workpage edit permissions in Work Zone to edit the workpage, add wizard layouts,
  • ProcessAutomationParticipant role collection
  • 'Execute' permission for shared environment (Share an Environment | SAP Help Portal)

Note: creating wizard layouts based on ‘guided processes’ is currently released in preview environments of SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition / SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and will become available in the non-preview environments with the subsequent release.

Auto-generated Wizard Structure

When selecting a guided process, the wizard layout is automatically generated based on the process definition, saving time and ensuring consistency.

Design Wizard with Widgets & UI Cards

Just like with a manually-created one, you can customize guided process-based wizard layouts using different widgets and UI cards, providing flexibility in designing the user experience.

  • Adding Widgets / Cards to each step: 
  • Example Step with Image widget:

Note: Already in a previous release, UI cards have been enhanced to interact with wizard layouts in SAP Build Work Zone, for instance to enforce users taking certain actions. Details and code examples for such UI cards are available via our central SAP Build Work Zone GitHub sample repository.

Process Instance Creation

When a user starts a wizard based on a guided process, an instance of that process is started automatically, allowing execution & resuming of the steps according to the defined process structure.

Conclusion – SAP Build Guided Experiences

This streamlined integration reduces development time and ensures that teams can leverage the power of SAP Build Process Automation alongside SAP Build Work Zone bringing exciting possibilities for creating powerful & content-rich guided experiences. These innovations not only simplify process design and execution but also ensure that you can deploy efficient, scalable guided experiences tailored to your unique needs – bringing different capabilities from SAP Build together via a deeply integrated, end-to-end experience!

What’s Next?

Looking ahead, we are planning to introduce additional features to further enhance guided experiences. These upcoming capabilities aim to add additional flexibility for creating, designing & operating guided experiences with SAP Build.  Here's a sneak peek at what's planned (*):

Spoiler
  • Repeating Guided Experiences
  • Starting Multiple Instances of the same Guided Experience
  • Support for more Step UIs, including Forms
  • Integration with Process Visibility for Monitoring
  • […]

In addition to those additional enhancements, we are also working on providing additional pre-built business content (packages) for guided experiences (*), such as onboarding in context of SAP SuccessFactors. Stay tuned as these new features roll out, offering even more ways to optimize your guided experiences and streamline your business processes!

(*) Disclaimer: Please note that the roadmap items shared here are subject to change and may not be delivered as initially planned. The information provided is for informational purposes only and does not constitute a commitment by SAP. As always, the best resource to learn about upcoming capabilities is the central roadmap explorer.

Additional Read

To learn more about the topic covered in this blog post, please also refer to the following assets & follow us on SAP community.

  • Related Blog Posts:
    • What’s new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
    • SAP Build Process Automation - Introducing Environments
  • Learning Journeys & Certification: 
    • Work Zone: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning
    • Process Automation: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Reference Architectures:
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-sap-build-work-zone
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-build-process-automation
  • SAP Help guides for more details:
    • Work Zone Preview: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-advanced-edition/sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/what... (will move to regular admin guide with next release)
    • Process Automation: Create a Guided Process
  • GitHub Repository for Work Zone Examples: https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration/tree/main/advanced/wizard

entry.title: Try it out now : SAP Dynamic Forms generally available now in SAP BTP Trail Account
entry.content:
SAP Dynamic Forms - Trial edition is a trial subscription to the SAP Dynamic Forms BTP Application, an all-in-one solution that allows users to create, manage, and use digital forms that change in real-time as they are being filled out. Designers will be guided through the steps required to create a data-driven and responsive form, and users will be guided through the steps required to complete forms based on these designs.

Documentation Link : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DYNAMIC_FORMS?locale=en-US

SAP Dynamic forms Feature Scope : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DYNAMIC_FORMS/f9acfd39418a47cab622574ea9acaf84/7f9667d3844b46e6a3a9e17989dc675c.html?locale=en-US

SAP BTP Trial Account : https://cockpit.hanatrial.ondemand.com/trial/#

References :: https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-dynamic-forms-1-0-released-features/ba-p/13748122

entry.title: How SAP manages its Early Talent programs – Get insights by joining our CEI project
entry.content:
SAPs Vocational Training (VT) department, responsible for training SAP’s apprentices and dual students, uses SAP SuccessFactors for its processes. However, there are apprentice and student specific processes not covered by SAP SuccessFactors, for which the VT department used custom solutions and many spreadsheets in the past. This caused redundancies, data flow gaps, a lack of transparency and a resource intensive reporting.

As dual students & apprentices ourselves, we wanted to solve those challenges and make our own lives easier, by developing a solution covering the unique requirements for early talent management and connecting it with the data from SAP SuccessFactors, providing a unified overview for students, trainers and managers alike.

Our internal Early Talent Management solution is built from the ground up on SAP Business Technology Platform, tightly integrated into SAP SuccessFactors, with now over 20 Fiori applications, covering most of the requirements of SAPs 14 VT programs from around the globe. We use the solution at SAP since mid 2022 and are currently evaluating if it could also be used to manage our interns.

With this Customer Engagement Initiative we’d like to share insights about the capabilities of our solution and evaluate if our internal solution might also be suitable for customer use cases in a potential future solution.

Form of activities

The final form of activities depends on the participants preferences. We currently plan:

  • Initial call (1h) in December 2024 to give an initial overview about the solutions capabilities as well as the timeline of the CEI project.
  • Throughout January till June 2025 deep dives (1-2h) into specific capability areas of the solution, including time for Q&A and feedback as well as discussing challenges you currently face. Our capability areas include:
    • Student & Cohort management
    • Work (Rotation) assignment & management
    • Conversion process for students into full-time positions
    • Academics & grade reporting by students
    • Extracurricular activities for students
    • Scientific papers & a small paper book library system
  • Furthermore, we’d like to offer two sessions (1-2h) focused on your current challenges in the following two areas to get a more holistic understanding of them:
    • Apprentice handling including the Apprentice report book (Berichtsheft)
    • Functionalities required for managing interns
  • Closing call (1h) in Q3 2025

Prerequisites

Customers and partners should use or plan to use SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and be familiar with apprenticeships, internships, co-op student or Vocational Training programs

Register for our CEI project ‘SAP Early Talent Management’ from October 14th till November 15th, 2024.

Once the registration process is finalized, we invite participants to the initial call to go through the project scope and timelines.

We are looking forward to meet you in December. 🙂

entry.title: How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP
entry.content:
Terry Penner and Jürgen Butsmann discuss the latest developments in SAP Business AI for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).  The conversation focuses on how Artificial Intelligence is evolving at SAP, with particular attention to large language models (LLMs) and their growing application in SAP cloud ERP and SAP BTP.

Listen to the podcast: Spotify | Apple Podcasts

Topics discussed:

Part 1: Evolution of AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

  • Latest developments in the AI market
  • SAP’s ERP strategy evolution for AI
  • Latest solution architecture updates

Part 2:  Differentiating the AI solutions in SAP cloud ERP

  • What is Joule
  • Differentiating AI offerings
  • How can AI add value to a business
  • Partner role with SAP Business AI

Part 3: Cloud ERP AI Priorities

  • SAP cloud ERP AI development priorities
  • SAP’s Business AI scenario priorities

This blog is a follow-up to AI for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: deep-dive discussion

About the speakers:

Jürgen Butsmann is part of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Marketing team responsible for AI in the context of SAP S/4HANA, finance and digital supply chain. Jürgen has been with SAP for more than 26 years.  He is a recognized expert for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP .

Terry Penner is part of the SAP BTP marketing team for Business Applications, focused on SAP BTP for SAP S/4HANA. He has more than 23 years of technical and business experience in the SAP platform and analytics, including working directly on hundreds of customer implementations.

Part 1: Evolution of AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

Latest developments in the AI market

Terry: In today's discussion, we'll explore how Artificial Intelligence for SAP S/4HANA and the SAP Business Technology Platform is evolving. Jürgen, what new developments have occurred in the AI market since we last spoke?

Jürgen: The evolution of technology continues unabated. We're observing an increase in services such as document services and advanced large language models (LLMs). The LLMs we see today are notably more fact-based, not just designed for entertainment purposes like the early iterations of ChatGPT, but for fact-oriented applications like those seen with Aleph Alpha. Additionally, there have been multiple iterations and diverse models emerging tailored to specific needs. We've also noticed a decline in pricing, which makes these AI services increasingly attractive and accessible in the market.

Terry: Absolutely. We've also witnessed significant legal developments, with content owners like The New York Times and others looking to withdraw their material from large language models, underscoring the importance of having robust and compliant data sources for these models.

SAP’s ERP strategy evolution for AI

Terry: How has SAP's ERP strategy for AI evolved?

Jürgen: Our strategy has evolved significantly, placing a more substantial emphasis on an AI-first approach across SAP. This shift means a lot of new ideas and pre-made implementations for our customers. We've taken a two-pronged approach in our investments. First, we're enhancing end-user productivity by integrating digital assistants into our systems, with Joule being a primary component. Although still early in deployment, these tools signify a shift from traditional graphical user interfaces to more intuitive digital assistant interactions.

Further confirming this direction, our CEO, Christian Klein, anticipates that in ten years, most system users will interact with digital assistants rather than traditional interfaces. Our focus also extends to natural language interactions within these systems, fostering intelligent activities and responses.

 

Secondly, we are concentrating on enhancing end-to-end processes. While we continue to enhance user interaction and productivity on the front end, significant efforts are directed towards optimizing large-scale processes, particularly within enterprise service management. This holistic approach ensures not just improvements in individual functionalities but also the acceleration of end-to-end process flows, all underpinned by advanced AI and generative AI technologies. This dual focus aligns with our commitment to transforming business processes through innovative AI applications.

Latest solution architecture updates

Terry: What are the latest updates to SAP's AI solution architecture since then?

Jürgen: One major change is the ongoing development of Joule. Initially, Joule had a rather rigid architecture that was not very flexible and did not support LLMs. This has changed. We have introduced the capability to create prompts using LLM support, which is a priority that has helped increase acceptance of this digital system and co-pilot for everyday work. Additionally, we've improved context sensitivity—knowing where you are in your system and what steps you are processing. This is a key differentiator from other co-pilots on the market and allows us to integrate responses that feel less artificial and more contextually aware.  It's able to  integrate proprietary documents into the context of the business processes.

Furthermore, we continue to develop its extensibility. We've opened possibilities for customers to tailor the co-pilot to perform functions specific to their needs.

Terry: And regarding extensibility, that’s a significant area where SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) collaborates with the ERP team by supplying developer tools and experiences, such as the vector engine within SAP HANA, to support these functionalities—it's a joint effort.

Part 2:  Differentiating the AI solutions in SAP cloud ERP

What is Joule?

Terry: We’ve spoken quite a bit about Joule. Could you clarify exactly what Joule is?

Jürgen: Essentially, Joule serves as an interface for natural language commands within our system architecture. It acts as an entry point where users can interact with the system to trigger functions or retrieve necessary business process information. While Joule leverages AI technologies, its use doesn’t alter the inherent capabilities of our AI. It fits seamlessly into our system, making those use cases distinct and noteworthy. Ideally, Joule simplifies user interactions by providing a natural, conversational interface with the system. While our systems could operate without Joule, using it enhances the user experience, making interactions more engaging and straightforward.

  

Differentiating the AI solutions in SAP cloud ERP

Terry: What's the difference with SAP Business AI? How should customers differentiate potential AI offerings?

Jürgen: The top priority for SAP Business AI is to provide clear and measurable value to customers. Before using AI scenarios, there must be a reasonable and measurable return on investment. The focus is on implementing AI in SAP systems without the need to change generic functionality, where the system's context details can be used to address processes more effectively.

The reliability of AI use cases is a major focus, based on fact-based foundation models whenever possible. All AI-assisted processes undergo an ethical check and are supported by the data security of SAP Business AI architecture.

We are also continuing to refine our pricing approach and evaluating if we will continue using current AI units or shift to different technologies in the future. Customers consider usage, potential value add, costs, and investments when determining the break-even point for their use cases. Moreover, customers expect our AI solutions to be user-friendly.

Terry: Adding to that, not all AI use cases deliver sufficient value for application. It's crucial to concentrate on scenarios with high-quality underlying data linked to large language models. For example, one effective use of AI I've observed involves searching through support cases and providing recommendations to new team members, enhancing the search experience and improving how information is interpreted and suggested.

How can AI add value to a business

Terry: How would you explain to a customer how AI can add value to a business?

Jürgen: When considering how AI supports business processes, the user's connection to the system is paramount. Ideally, this happens through a digital co-pilot, like Joule in our case. Users interact with Joule by posing questions that prompt queries to a large language model, which in turn provides responses. However, large language models may lack specific business process details since those are unique and not universally predefined, leading to potentially incomplete answers. 

 

To address this, we might extend our architecture to incorporate calls to the ERP system or access other proprietary data, ensuring no sensitive business information is exposed to potentially vulnerable cloud environments. We balance accessibility with security by using a vector database to locate and integrate the right business-specific data from outside the large language model's scope.

As users submit human language requests, these are processed through the digital system and converted into technical prompts by a large language model. These prompts trigger the vector engine to retrieve the necessary information. The returning data can then be refined further by the large language models, which are all deployed securely within our system environment to prevent data leakage.

The data retrieval process benefits from enhancements such as semantic similarities and pattern recognition to continuously improve response accuracy. Once processed, the information is delivered back to the user, completing the cycle and delivering the value we aim for with our AI-enabled systems.

This systematic approach ensures that AI not only supports but also enhances business processes effectively while maintaining data integrity and security.

Terry: So, we could describe Joule as the front-end experience, distinct from the backend LLM that powers the AI functionalities.

Partner role with SAP Business AI

Terry: What role do partners play in this space?

Jürgen: Firstly, our partners have a deep understanding of our customers. It's crucial that partners are well-versed in our setup, strategy, architecture, pricing, and other elements that shape how we envision utilizing these services. We leverage our partners to create scalable solutions that precisely address specific customer needs. They are instrumental in implementing individual projects that tackle unique business challenges at the customer sites. Our partners have a proven track record of handling such use cases effectively.

Part 3: AI Extensibility and SAP BTP

SAP cloud ERP AI development priorities

Terry: What are some of the other AI development priorities at SAP cloud ERP, and how is SAP BTP supporting those AI scenarios?

 

Jürgen: We're integrating various technologies beyond what we've already discussed, like our work with live language models. For example, merging our capabilities with Microsoft Copilot offers expanded possibilities for enhancing natural language interactions. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and the AI Foundation are pivotal in anchoring these flexibilities. This integration allows us to broaden our scope and reinforce the support we provide for cloud ERP AI scenarios.

SAP’s Business AI scenario priorities

Terry: What business scenarios are central to SAP's Business AI focus?

Jürgen: Our focus isn't narrow, but we do see significant interest particularly in the finance sector. Currently, we're engaging extensively with businesses, customers, and partners to pinpoint key priorities specific to finance. Our approach begins by assessing what immediate actions we can take and then contemplating more visionary possibilities for the future. For example, one idea is the automatic creation of forecast scenarios based on historical data within our systems. This capability could enhance management, strategy, and decision-making meetings significantly, even without all finance experts gathered at one table. Additionally, there are numerous other aspects of finance where AI can play a transformative role.

Terry: We will link to some specific use cases within the podcast notes. Thank you, Jürgen, for joining us and sharing your insights into AI for cloud ERP and the business technology platform. It's been enlightening.

Jürgen: Thank you for having me. I hope our discussion helps people understand better where we are now and where we are headed with our technology.

[ Note:  This blog was adapted from the podcast transcript by an SAP system using Azure OpenAI–gpt-4 ]

Key links:

  • Four ways to infuse business-ready, trusted AI into your processes
  • SAP BTP for S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Business AI

Read more in this series:

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

entry.title: SuccessConnect 2024-Every employee is a success story with SAP BTP
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform at SuccessConnect 2024

Exciting news, demos and information on innovation, integration, extending HR apps, AI, analytics and workforce planning

It’s time to get ready for this year’s SAP SuccessFactors SuccessConnect event!  This year’s sold-out event takes place in Lisbon, Portugal October 28 through the 30th and features more SAP Business Technology Platform-related sessions than ever before – 18 sessions including one special session on workforce planning available for free in the virtual event!

Discover solutions designed to fuel employee and business success and strategies for improving your employee experiences, decisions, and processes.

To help guide attendees in packing their agendas with as much SAP BTP content and information as possible, here’s a guide of what is happening. Add these sessions to your SuccessConnect agenda now!

For more information on this year’s SuccessConnect event, visit https://www.sap.com/events/successconnect.html or to learn more about how SAP Business Technology Platform can help HR visit http://www.sap.com/btp-for-hr .

General and Business Technology Platform

  • Using SAP Integration Suite to connect HR with the business
    Learn how SAP Integration Suite can help you connect HR across the business by integrating SAP SuccessFactors HCM with other SAP and third-party applications. Hear how organizations of all sizes are using this approach to create a single source of people data, enable end-to-end processes, and drive automation and efficiency. SAP Integration Suite 

  • Using SAP Build to extend your SAP SuccessFactors solutions
    Standardized business processes have come a long way, but every company still has a few things it does differently from everybody else. SAP Build solutions can help extend your human capital management solutions to address your specific business needs. See firsthand how to create low-code/no-code apps that boost end-user adoption and efficiency. SAP Build 

  • Road map and vision: Technology Foundation
    Explore the latest innovations and enhancements in the technology foundation of SAP SuccessFactors solutions, including workforce analytics, security, integration, extensibility, and more. Gain insights into what we have planned for our upcoming releases and get your questions answered by our product experts.

  • See how new innovations help you integrate and extend your SAP SuccessFactors solutions
    Discover how our recent innovations around domain-driven APIs and push events can help transform your business operations and enhance the user experience. Learn how you can use these technologies to achieve near real-time integrations with external service providers and streamline your business processes

  • Build trust with security and identity management technologies
    Discover the key technologies and innovations to help you secure your people data. Explore key challenges and learn how to mitigate risk and develop trust with a deep dive into newly available capabilities, including customer-managed keys in the new advanced encryption add-on and enhancements to the role-based permissions tool.

  • Unlock innovation with the generative AI hub
    The new generative AI hub in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad makes it easier for developers to build applications securely and efficiently with access to a broad range of tools and large language models. See how tools for prompt engineering, experimentation, and other AI capabilities can accelerate your development of applications for HR and more.

 

Analytics and Workforce Planning

  • Hands-on-lab: Create compelling report stories in SAP SuccessFactors HCM
    Designed for reporting administrators, this session will help you learn how to use a single, unified reporting approach to tell compelling stories. Get hands-on experience with key capabilities, such as rich visualizations and intelligent data joins, and learn how to use live data from SAP SuccessFactors solutions. This session is intended for intermediate to advanced reporting administrators with a working knowledge of the report center and role-based security permissions. It is for existing customers only. Laptops and scripts will be supplied. Note that registration is required for this session. Space is limited so that speakers have time to answer attendee questions. If a registered session attendee is five minutes late to the session, we will give their seat to the next person waiting in line. SAP Analytics Cloud 

  • Road map and vision: Reporting and analytics
    Explore the latest innovations in analytics and the significant benefits they offer for your business. Discover how to make use of these enhancements by finding out more about our new SAP SuccessFactors Analytics solution, report stories within SAP SuccessFactors solutions, and workforce planning in SAP Analytics Cloud for planning. SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 

  • Improving people sustainability with workforce planning and data analytics
    With skill gaps increasing and regulatory mandates growing more complex, CHROs are turning to longer-term people sustainability. Discover how HR leaders can use AI-driven analytics and strategic workforce planning to create a workforce of the future. Gain insights on using skills analysis to inform talent investments and improve workforce planning. SAP Analytics Cloud 

  • Get the mot out of workforce planning templates using SAP Analytics Cloud for planning
    SAP Analytics Cloud for planning includes workforce planning templates. Get an overview of the various templates available in the business content network and the planning processes they support. Whether you’re looking to do operational, strategic, or skill-based workforce planning, these insights can help you. SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 

  • Top three ways to create dynamic operational report stories
    How do you best create stories as unfolding narratives for your users to further explore data in more detail? Gain practical tips on how to use report stories within SAP SuccessFactors solutions to create dynamic, interactive stories that let users dive into details as needed. SAP Analytics Cloud 

 

Customer Speaking Sessions

  • How Eurobank uses SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking and SAP BTP to comply with local labor legislation
    To maintain compliance with a new labor law and avoid costly fines, Eurobank chose the SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking solution and SAP Business Technology Platform. Learn how the financial organization accurately tracks employee work hours, shifts, and overtime while linking data in real time to third-party platforms.

  • Unleashing HR insights: Reporting and workforce analytics at Dr. Oetker
    See how reporting in SAP SuccessFactors solutions has transformed Dr. Oetker’s HR reporting landscape and explore real-world use cases in the SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Analytics solution. From overcoming challenges to unlocking transformative insights, discover the power of reporting and analytics capabilities of SAP solutions. SAP Analytics Cloud 

  • How agile HR transformation at Volkswagen increased performance and transparency
    Using the “SAFe” framework, Volkswagen implemented SAP SuccessFactors solutions in one of the largest HR transformation programs worldwide. Join this session to learn how the company established a new product organization to increase performance and transparency.

Demo stations

  • DP106: HR analytics and workforce planning area
    Discover how you can improve company performance and decision-making with data-driven people insights, workforce planning tools, and AI-driven recommendations. SAP Analytics Cloud  SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 

  • DP105: System administration and extensibility area
    See how SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) can help you grow your capabilities with third-party integrations and custom extensions featuring a real-world demo of a re-envisioning automated equipment ordering with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central driving real-time triggers to order new hire equipment in SAP Ariba via SAP Integration Suite with automated service ticket create in SAP Service Cloud. SAP Integration Suite  SAP Build

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 Announcements - Consolidated and Simplified
entry.content:
In this blog, I have consolidated everything related to SAP TechEd 2024 in one place! Hope this will help the community.

Note: Don't miss the list of all the links of news, recordings, source-code, demo etc. towards the end of this blog.

 

First let's have a quick summary of all the key announcements.

 

 

Let's go in detail.

SAP Build + ABAP Cloud

  • SAP Build unifies the entire development and extension capabilities and will now also include ABAP Cloud.
  • Technically, it means that SAP Build will integrate ABAP development tools and ABAP development environments for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP BTP.
  • Developers will also be able to create and monitor their ABAP Cloud projects directly from SAP Build.
  • It will be available by Q4 2024.
  • SAP Build Lobby will be the central starting point for all extension projects.

Demo of SAP Build Lobby

 

SAP Build for S/4HANA Extension Development

  • A new extensibility wizard is available now which will let developers access SAP Build directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
  • With this, developers can extend custom fields, business logic and processes either on-stack (deployed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP environment) or side-by-side (deployed in SAP BTP).
  • In Q4 2024, the extensibility wizard will let developers create custom SAP Fiori and SAPUI5 applications, leveraging the full set of features available in SAP Build.

 

New Generative AI Capabilities of SAP Build with Joule

  • New capabilities in SAP Build Code such as code explanation and documentation search will reduce development time for Java and JavaScript developers (Q4 2024).
  • Developers will also be able to automate workflows in SAP Build Process Automation with assistance from generative AI (Q4 2024)
  • ABAP developers will be able to generate high-quality code and unit tests with Joule that comply with SAP’s ABAP Cloud Development model, and also generate explanations for legacy code, making it easier to modernize legacy codebases and migrate to a clean core (Q1 2025)
  • Developers will also be able to enhance Joule with custom skills using Joule studio in SAP Build, integrating it seamlessly with both SAP and non-SAP applications (Q1 2025)

For more information on SAP Build, refer to SAP Build.

 

Joule with SAP Build Work Zone

  • Joule will also be integrated into SAP Build Work Zone (Q4 2024)
  • It will be available in SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, the SAP Start site and the SAP Mobile Start app.

 

Joule with Collaborative AI Agents

  • SAP will infuse Joule with multiple autonomous AI agents that will combine their unique expertise to collaboratively accomplish complex workflows (Q4 2024)
  • With an in-built agentic capability, Joule can now solve complex business problems for you through planning, self-reflection, logical reasoning and collaboration.
  • Developers will be able to extend Joule by building their own autonomous AI agent use cases with SAP Build (Q1 2025)

Joule Agent Demo

 

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Joule will be available for all SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers (Q4 2024)
  • Joule capabilities will expand to support key functions including sales professionals, warehouse clerks and project managers (Q1 of 2025)

 

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • Following the introduction of Joule into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in July, SAP launched a feature pack with new use cases that let business users instruct Joule with simple commands.
  • The feature pack includes a new implementation wizard that installs a model outlining criteria to evaluate extensions built with low-code, no-code and pro-code tools on SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP.
  • Joule will be incorporated into the top 300 most-frequently-used SAP Fiori apps (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will also be able to take master data change requests within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, so users don’t have to navigate across multiple menus.

 

Joule in SAP BTP

  • Joule will be available to use with SAP HANA Cloud in SAP BTP cockpit (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be incorporated into SAP BTP cockpit (Q4 2024)
  • Joule can be directed to perform transactional scenarios like creating a subaccount, onboarding users, and answering questions about SAP BTP administrations in general. 

 

Joule in Other SAP Solutions

  • Joule will be available in the SAP Service Cloud solution and SAP Concur solutions (Q1 2025)
  • SAP now offers Joule across the SAP Integrated Business Planning solution, the SAP Digital Manufacturing solution, and the SAP Asset Performance Management application.
  • SAP will continue to roll out Joule across the entire SAP Supply Chain portfolio.
  • Joule will be available in the SAP Sales Cloud solution with navigational and task-management assistance (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will help SAP LeanIX solutions users manage and optimize their IT landscape (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be available with SAP Signavio (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be available in SAP Mobile Start (Q4 2024)      

 

Joule studio in SAP Build

  • SAP will release Joule studio in SAP Build (Q4 2024).
  • Joule studio will provide a dedicated environment for businesses to create, deploy, monitor and manage custom skills for Joule developed with low-code, no-code capabilities.
  • Custom skills, which complement Joule’s out-of-the-box capabilities from SAP, extend conversational AI to organization-specific workflows.
  • Additionally, customers can now integrate any third-party system into Joule for an entirely integrated conversational user experience.

 

SAP Knowledge Graph

  • SAP announces the addition of a knowledge graph engine to SAP HANA Cloud (Q1 2025)
  • The knowledge graph engine will let users identify and understand complex relationships in their data not otherwise discoverable using traditional data modeling tools.
  • SAP announces SAP Knowledge Graph, a new solution that will unlock the full value of SAP data by connecting it with the rich business context captured in SAP applications.
  • Accessible through SAP Datasphere and Joule in Q1 2025, it will help root AI in SAP-specific business semantics, reducing the risk of inaccurate or irrelevant results.
  • In its initial release, this solution will focus on SAP S/4HANA, providing a semantic layer that connects over 450,000 ABAP tables, 80,000 CDS views and thousands of SAP Fiori applications, along with OData APIs and entity sets.

 

SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine

  • Knowledge graph engine will be added to SAP HANA Cloud (Q1 2025)
  • By combining the vector engine and the knowledge graph engine, you can harness the power of structured and unstructured data together.

 

SAP Expands Strategic AI Partnerships

  • Anthropic: SAP introduces Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet in Amazon Bedrock to generative AI hub.
  • HCLTech: HCLTech announces a newly built AI Center of Excellence in Munich focused on developing AI use cases for the AI Foundation on SAP BTP.
  • IBM: SAP announces the addition of IBM Granite, IBM’s flagship series of transparent and efficient LLMs, to the growing repository of AI models available in generative AI hub.
  • Meta: SAP continues to execute its multi-vendor strategy for LLMs with the addition of Meta Llama 3.1 70B, which is built to deliver performance and cost efficiency.
  • Mistral AI: SAP continues to expand its partnership with Mistral AI by adding Mistral Large 2 to generative AI hub.
  • PwC: PwC announces it is using generative AI capabilities based on SAP’s generative AI hub.

 

Generative AI Hub + ABAP

  • Developers will be able to access generative AI hub capabilities from custom ABAP applications using the ABAP AI SDK powered by the intelligent scenario lifecycle management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud functionality. 
  • Using the ABAP AI SDK, ABAP developers can easily infuse AI capabilities into their custom applications and extensions.

 

Generative AI Hub – New LLMs Support

New LLMs will be available for generative AI hub by the end of 2024, including:

  • Aleph Alpha Pharia-1
  • Amazon Titan Image Generator
  • IBM Granite
  • Mistral Large 2
  • OpenAI Dall-E 3

 

Generative AI Hub – New Data Centers

New data centers for generative AI hub by the end of Q4 2024:

  • AWS: Singapore
  • Google Cloud Platform: Australia (Sydney); Japan (Osaka)
  • Microsoft Azure: Australia (Sydney); Switzerland (Zurich)

 

Generative AI Hub – Other Updates

  • We will be able to integrate advanced AI capabilities into web apps with new SDK support for ABAP, Java, and JavaScript in generative AI hub (Q4 2024)
  • Orchestration improvements to the generative AI hub capability in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad that will enable document grounding and indexing pipelines (Q4 2024)

 

New generative AI functionality for customers move to the cloud faster

  • SAP announced a new generative AI functionality that helps customers move to the cloud faster (Q1 2025).
  • Accessed through the SAP Cloud ALM solution, customers and consultants will be able to get expert answers, proactive guidance, and task automation aligned with the RISE with SAP Methodology.

 

SAP Expands its Portfolio of Learning and Certification Offerings

  • SAP launches new role-based certifications, free training materials and hands-on opportunities for developers and tech professionals.
  • These includes 60 new self-paced learning journeys, trial experiences and solution demonstrations that help tech professionals benefit from the latest fully integrated and practical training.

 

SAP Datasphere

  • SAP announced new embedded data lake capabilities for SAP Datasphere (Q4 2024)
  • Built on SAP HANA Cloud SQL on files and data lake file service capabilities, it will expand the business data fabric architecture with a data lake option, complementing existing storage solutions.
  • This helps businesses access, manage, and analyze data across cloud and hybrid environments while preserving its context and logic, improving decision-making, operational efficiency, and AI-driven innovation.

 

New embedded data lake capabilities

  • An integrated object store, which provides a simpler way to store large amounts of data, making it easier for businesses to expand their storage as needed.
  • Spark compute, which facilitates efficient data transformation and processing based on existing SAP Datasphere data integration capabilities.
  • SQL on files functionality, which lets developers access data in the integrated object store without needing to physically copy the data.

These capabilities let users import and integrate data at scale while maintaining a consistent user experience that ensures seamless data integration from both SAP sources (including SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Warehouse) and non-SAP sources while preserving the original structure and context of the data.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

  • SAP announced general availability of the new SAP Analytics Cloud compass feature (Q1 2025)
  • SAP Analytics Cloud compass feature can be used to model complex risk scenarios and simulate a broad range of probable outcomes.

 

SAP Integration Suite

  • SAP Integration Suite, starter edition will offer an unlimited number of prepackaged SAP integrations and a fixed number of customizable integrations for GROW with SAP and SAP SuccessFactors customers (Q4 2024)
  • New integration adapters will allow users manage data from various third-party technology platforms, including Anaplan, Coupa, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more.
  • AI supported custom script optimization (2025)
  • AI support iFlow creation is now Generally Available

 

SAP TechEd 2024 - Recordings and News

SAP TechEd keynote replay - https://www.youtube.com/live/VUp4nt3zNCg

SAP TechEd News Guide 2024 - https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide.html

Joule with Collaborative Capabilities - TechEd Announement - https://news.sap.com/2024/10/sap-teched-copilot-joule-collaborative-capabilities-enterprise-ai/

SAP Transforms Joule with Collaborative AI Agents - https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/ai.html#sap-transforms-joule-with-collaborative-ai-agents

SAP Knowledge Graph to Unlock Business Context Across SAP Ecosystem - https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/ai.html#sap-knowledge-graph-unlock-business-context-across-sap-ecosystem

Cloud ERP announcements - https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/cloud-erp.html

Data and Analytics announcements - https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/data-and-analytics.html

List of GitHub Repositories for SAP TechEd Hands-on Sessions

Use SAP Integration Suite with SAP IBP and third-party systems - https://github.com/SAP-samples/teched2024-IN280
