Hope this will help! Let me know if I have missed anything important 😊

entry.title: IBM Granite.13b.chat Large Language Model- Now GA on the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
SAP’s generative AI Hub is the central place for BTP application developers to access and use generative AI tools and models, and developers can now choose IBM Granite as their large language model (LLM). This article will explain some of Granite’s unique characteristics and then show you how to access and use it.

IBM Granite.13b.chat is a decoder, English-only, enterprise-grade LLM and part of a series of IBM foundation models for generative artificial intelligence tasks. IBM stands behind Granite LLMs with IP indemnification protection according to its Standard Terms and Conditions, to provide users with peace of mind. Granite.13b.chat was trained on enterprise-relevant content that meets rigorous data governance, regulatory, and risk criteria defined and enforced by the IBM AI Ethics Code and Chief Privacy Office. You can read the details here: https://www.ibm.com/downloads/cas/X9W4O6BM. Granite.13b.chat delivers high accuracy for retrieval augmented generation (RAG), summarization, content generation, named entity recognition, insight extraction, and classification.

A considerable portion of Granite training data is from business domains. For business-specific tasks in domains such as finance, some IBM performance benchmarks suggest that the accuracy of Granite models is on par with larger models (70B parameter+) at lower latency and, due to its smaller size (13B parameter), lower infrastructure spending.

AI capabilities in SAP AI Core & SAP AI Launchpad that utilize the generative AI hub can leverage Granite.13b.chat.

Get started by:

  1. Learning how to use foundational models on SAP AI Core (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-consumption-llm.html), and
  2. learning how to prompt Granite.13b.chat (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/docs/content/wsj/analyze-data/fm-models-ibm-chat.html?context=wx&audience=wdp).

For more information about Granite.13b.chat and instruction-tuning details, see the model card (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/login?context=wx&sign_in_target=%2Fwx%2Fsamples%2Fmodels%2Fibm%2Fgranite-13b-chat-v2%3Fcontext%3Dwx%3Fcontext%3Dwx%26audience%3Dwdp).

entry.title: SAP BTP: The Foundation for Intelligent Enterprise with SAP Business AI
entry.content:
The digital revolution is in full swing, and at its core lies the transformative power of artificial intelligence (AI). For the tech-savvy enterprise, AI is not just a buzzword, but a strategic imperative. SAP Business AI emerges as a key enabler of this transformation, offering a robust suite of tools that empower businesses to optimize operations, automate processes, and gain a competitive edge.

SAP: A Legacy of Innovation in the Enterprise Landscape

Since its inception in 1972, SAP has been a pioneer in enterprise technology, guiding businesses through technological shifts and helping them leverage new opportunities. With SAP Business AI, SAP continue this tradition, delivering a cutting-edge platform that harnesses the power of AI to redefine business processes.

SAP Business AI: More Than Just Automation

SAP Business AI represents a paradigm shift in how businesses leverage data and technology. It's not just about automating tasks; it's about fundamentally changing how businesses operate and innovate. Think of it as an intelligent layer embedded within your existing SAP landscape, constantly learning and adapting to optimize processes and drive better outcomes.

Key Components and Applications: A Technical Perspective

SAP Business AI is deeply integrated across SAP's entire product portfolio, providing seamless access to AI capabilities within the context of everyday business processes. Here's a closer look at the technical components that make it so powerful:

Across key areas like Finance, Supply Chain, HR, Procurement, Marketing & Commerce, Sales & Services, and IT. This broad reach allows businesses to leverage AI in every aspect of their operations.

SAP Business AI provides a dual approach to AI, offering both pre-built AI services and the tools to build custom AI solutions. For specific business processes, SAP offers ready-to-use AI services that can be easily adapted to unique workflows. These services cover areas such as document processing, data attribute recommendations, and Robotic Process Automation.

At the heart of SAP Business AI lies a robust data foundation. This foundation includes SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) as the underlying infrastructure, along with services like SAP Datasphere, Analytics Cloud, and Integration Suite. These tools make it easy to connect, model, and analyze data across different applications, ensuring that AI algorithms have access to the high-quality data they need.

SAP also recognizes the importance of collaboration in the AI ecosystem. Establishing strategic partnerships with other industry leaders, such as Microsoft, Google, AWS, and IBM, to leverage their expertise and technologies. This collaborative approach ensures that SAP Business AI remains at the forefront of AI innovation, so that with leading AI vendors and large language model (LLM) providers, giving customers access to the latest innovations and ensuring flexibility in their AI deployments.

SAP Business AI Approach: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible

SAP's approach to AI is guided by three core principles, as highlighted in the image: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible.

Relevant: SAP Business AI is designed to be relevant from day one. Its deep integration with SAP applications ensures that AI capabilities are readily available and easy to use within the context of everyday business processes.

Reliable: SAP has a long-standing reputation for delivering reliable enterprise software, and this commitment extends to SAP Business AI. The platform is built on a foundation of high-quality data, rigorous testing, and robust security measures, ensuring that businesses can trust its accuracy and performance.

Responsible: SAP recognizes the ethical implications of AI and is committed to developing and deploying AI in a responsible manner. This includes addressing concerns around bias, discrimination, and privacy, and ensuring that AI is used to augment human capabilities, not replace them.

Assessing Business AI Infused Across the SAP Product Portfolio

AI is already deeply ingrained in SAP's enterprise applications and business technology, used by tens of thousands of their cloud customers. AI-powered capabilities, based on proprietary deep learning and machine learning algorithms, are driving significant business value across SAP's cloud solutions. These capabilities include intelligent document processing, personalized recommendations, natural language processing, forecasting, and more. This means that businesses can leverage AI capabilities within their existing SAP solutions, without having to implement separate AI tools or platforms.

Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio

Transforming Business Applications with Generative AI

Generative AI is ushering in a new era of possibilities for business software. It enables the creation of new content, summarization of complex information, automated code writing, and much more. This new generation of AI can reason through business problems and offer solutions that were previously unimaginable.
SAP customers can expect to see tangible value from generative AI use cases across the entire SAP portfolio: Generative AI at SAP 

 Let's explore how generative AI is transforming key areas within the SAP ecosystem:

  • Cloud ERP: Streamlining processes like the verification of delivery notes, automating document processing with greater accuracy and efficiency. For example, imagine automatically processing thousands of invoices with minimal human intervention, using AI to extract relevant data and match it with purchase orders.
  • Human Capital Management: Freeing up managers' time by automating tasks like job postings, candidate sourcing, and performance reviews, while also reducing bias in these processes. AI can help identify the best candidates for a job by analyzing resumes and social media profiles, personalize onboarding experiences by providing tailored content and recommendations, and even predict employee attrition by identifying patterns in employee data.
  • Spend Management & Business Network: Empowering category managers with the information they need to make strategic procurement decisions, leveraging market dynamics and cost structure insights. AI can analyze spending patterns, identify cost-saving opportunities, and optimize supplier relationships by analyzing market data and predicting price fluctuations.
  • Customer Relationship Management: Elevating customer engagement by providing sales teams with personalized insights into customer preferences and purchase history, leading to tailored interactions and improved customer satisfaction. Imagine AI-powered chatbots that provide instant support and personalized recommendations to customers, improving customer satisfaction and reducing support costs. AI can also help sales teams prioritize leads, personalize marketing campaigns, and identify upselling and cross-selling opportunities.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Simplifying application development by enabling AI-powered code generation and test script creation, allowing developers to focus on more complex tasks. This can significantly reduce development time and costs, allowing businesses to innovate faster. Tools like SAP Build Code help automate code generation and testing, reducing development time and costs. For example, a developer could describe a desired functionality in natural language, and SAP Build Code would generate the corresponding code automatically.

Recognizing the Integration of SAP BTP with Business AI

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) serves as the underlying platform for SAP Business AI, providing the infrastructure and tools needed to build and deploy AI-powered applications.

Here are some build-in components of SAP BTP that are particularly relevant for AI development:

  • SAP Build Code: This tool empowers developers to build applications faster with the help of generative AI. The Joule copilot provides intelligent code suggestions and automates repetitive coding tasks, increasing developer productivity. For example, Joule can automatically generate code for common tasks, such as creating user interfaces or connecting to databases.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: This no-code solution enables business users to automate workflows and processes without writing any code. Its intuitive drag-and-drop interface and AI capabilities make it easy to create and deploy automations. This empowers business users to automate tasks without relying on IT, increasing agility and efficiency.
  • SAP HANA Cloud: This powerful in-memory database is optimized for handling large volumes of data and complex analytical queries, making it ideal for AI applications. Its ability to process data in memory allows for real-time analysis and insights, which is crucial for many AI use cases.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud: This cloud-based analytics platform provides a comprehensive suite of tools for data visualization, analysis, and planning. The integration of Joule, SAP's generative AI copilot, further enhances the platform's capabilities, enabling users to gain deeper insights from their data. For example, users can ask Joule to "show me the top 10 customers by revenue" or "predict next quarter's sales".

Focusing on the future of AI in the enterprise

SAP Business AI is more than just a technology; it's a strategic enabler for the modern enterprise. By seamlessly integrating AI across its product portfolio, SAP is empowering businesses to optimize processes, automate tasks, gain valuable insights, and ultimately achieve better outcomes. With a strong commitment to relevance, reliability, and responsibility, SAP Business AI is paving the way for a future where businesses can harness the full potential of AI in an ethical and sustainable manner.

Ready to delve deeper into the world of SAP Business AI? Here are some resources to get you started:

  • Artificial­ Intelligence­ |­ SAP ­Business ­AI
  • AI Solutions on SAP Business Technology Platform
  • AI Ethics | SAP Global AI Ethics Policy
  • SAP ­Community:­ Artificial­ Intelligence­ and­ Machine­ Learning­ at­ SAP
  • SAP ­Learning:­ Tutorials­ for­ SAP ­Developers­
  • Discover SAP Business AI features
  • Implement your use cases on SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Learning: how to use the SAP AI Core service on SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: Authorization Management Service in SAP Cloud Identity Service
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) is transitioning from using XSUAA for authorization management to a more integrated solution with Authorization Management Service (AMS). AMS is a pivotal component within the SAP Cloud Identity Service, designed to streamline and centralize the management of end-user authorizations for applications. This service ensures that individuals have appropriate access to the resources and functionalities they need to perform their roles effectively while mitigating security risks.

Firstly, many applications define authorizations internally to meet their specific domain needs. These internal mechanisms ensure tailored security but can lead to a decentralized and fragmented access management system. To address this, SAP Cloud Identity Services offers a solution by centralizing user assignments to roles and groups, significantly simplifying the management of user access.

The Authorization Management Service builds on this by providing a unified platform for managing authorizations across applications within SAP BTP. AMS integrates seamlessly with SAP Cloud Identity Services, enabling administrators to configure and assign policies through a centralized console. This integration enhances the efficiency and consistency of policy application across different services and users.

At the core of AMS is policy assignment. Within SAP Cloud Identity, each policy aligns with a group in the Identity Directory, and these policies can be linked to users by assigning them to the appropriate group. SAP offers flexibility in this process, allowing customers to use both predefined SAP policies and custom policies, which can be managed either through the Cloud Identity console or programmatically via the SCIM API. This ensures a scalable and adaptable authorization management system tailored to organizational needs. 

 

By centralizing authorization management, AMS offers several key benefits:

  • Improved Efficiency: Streamlined processes for assigning and managing authorizations reduce administrative overhead.
  • Enhanced Security: Centralized control over access helps prevent unauthorized access and data breaches.
  • Simplified Compliance: AMS can assist organizations in meeting regulatory requirements related to data privacy and security.
  • Flexibility: Customers can choose from pre-defined SAP policies or create custom policies to suit their specific needs.

What is a policy? 

A policy in the context of Authorization Management Service (AMS) is a structured set of rules that governs whether a user or group of users is allowed to perform specific actions on a resource, depending on certain conditions. These conditions can be based on user attributes or data from the business application.

In AMS, policies externalize authorization decisions to the AMS runtime engine, which evaluates relevant policies for a particular authorization request. Each policy defines the type of access (such as "Read") to a specific resource (like a business object) and is evaluated at runtime based on conditions set by administrators, who can adjust restrictions as needed. This allows for flexible and dynamic control over resource access within business applications.

Here are some example policies in Data Control Language (DCL) with actions on special resources where additionally attribute values restrict the user authorization. 

In the following sections, we will delve deeper into the functionalities of AMS, including its integration with SAP Cloud Identity Services, policy assignment mechanisms, and how it contributes to overall application security. 

Authorization Management for Cloud Identity Service

The Authorization Management Service (AMS) is now integrated into the user management console of SAP Cloud Identity, enabling customers to implement fine-grained access control for delegated administration. This feature allows organizations to restrict the authorizations of user management administrators to a specific subset of users or attributes within the user base. As a result, customers can delegate administrative tasks to local or specialized administrators without granting full access to all user data.

In some cases, the default administrator authorizations provided in the SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant may be too broad, offering unrestricted data access. For scenarios requiring more nuanced control, such as attribute-based access control (ABAC), customers can define custom authorization models with detailed instance-level restrictions. Administrators create authorization policies using user attributes and assign these policies to other administrators, enabling one administrator to manage only a specific subset of users or user attributes. Similarly, administrators can define policies using application organization IDs to restrict access to certain applications.

By leveraging policies, customers can configure precise access control for SAP Cloud Identity Services administrators. To activate this feature, it must first be enabled via the SAP Cloud Identity admin console. Once enabled, the "Authorization Policies" tab will appear within approximately 60 seconds in the administration console.

Initially, only the base policies are visible. You can create new authorization policies on the base policies and assign them to administrators. The custom policies will also appear in the list along with the base policies. 

1. Configure User Authorizations

Let me show you an example that how to define a policy and assign the policy to an administrator. Sue Smith is an administrator of SAP Cloud Identity Services, and she is responsible for the branch office in the USA. As such she needs to have access only to the users in the USA. When you create a new policy, you can restrict the users on the basis of the following attributes: user.name, country, costCenter, division, department, and organization. In this example, you can choose country to restrict management of the user only in the USA.

Note: The addresses must be marked as primary via the Identity Directory SCIM REST API. Users who don't have a primary address are excluded even if the user.addresses.country attribute matches the address of the user.

 Once the rule definition is completed, you can assign this policy to Sue Smith. 

Last but not least, you also need to remove the Read Users and Manage Users authorizations that Sue has as an administrator. 

2. Configure Application Authorizations

You can also configure new policies to allow the administrator manage a subset of applications in SAP Cloud Identity Services. When you create a new policy, you can restrict the users on the basis of the attribute application.organization. By default, all applications are in the global organization. You can edit the Organization ID of the application and define a custom policy. 

Authorization Management for Custom Business Applications

The Authorization Management Service (AMS) as part of SAP Cloud Identity Services (SCI) provides libraries and tools that enable developers of cloud business applications to define, enforce, and manage instance-based authorization checks. When integrated with the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model, AMS policies can include CAP roles along with additional filter criteria for instance-based authorizations that can be defined in the CAP model. These authorizations are then transformed into AMS policies, which can later be refined by authorization administrators in the SCI administration console and assigned to business users.

For developers, managing authorizations is a crucial but often complex task. Proper authorization ensures that end users have controlled access to critical data and functionality, protecting the company from potential harm caused by mistakes or malicious actions. However, finding the right balance of access control can be challenging and time-consuming.

The new Authorization Management Service simplifies this process for developers working on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). AMS provides a centralized system for managing end-user authorizations for business applications built on SAP BTP. It seamlessly integrates with SAP's development models, particularly the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model. When defining an authorization policy, developers can specify the conditions under which access is granted and whether these conditions can be adjusted later. Once the application is deployed on SAP BTP, administrators can further customize these authorization policies with specific conditions based on the business context, allowing for centralized, fine-tuned access control through a user-friendly interface.

Augment SAP BTP applications with AMS

The following steps are necessary to develop a CAP application with AMS:

  1. Add the latest version of @sap/ams and @sap/ams-dev dependencies to package.json.
  2. Annotate your services and entities with standard @requires and @restrict CAP annotations for role-based access control (RBAC).
  3. (Optional) Add @ams.publicFields annotations to be able to write attribute-based access control (ABAC) policies in Data Control Language (DCL), the AMS-specific language used to express authorization policies.
  4. (Optional) Use @ams.alias annotations to decouple your entity design decisions from abstract resources used in your policies. This leaves you free to change your entities later without affecting existing policies.
  5. Run your application with cds watch. The ams development plugin (@sap/ams-dev) will compile your cds model along with the AMS annotations to Data Control Language (DCL). Alternatively, generate the DCL files manually via cds build --for ams (or during a full cds build).

    Now, you can see that in gen/srv/ams/dcl/cap/basePolicies.dcl the base policy admin and Reader is generated. A user with the policy cap.admin can access the endpoints of the AdminService, a user with the policy cap.Reader can see all the books from the CatalogService. 

    You can observe that a folder with name ams is generated in your root folder. This is where we can define our schema extensions and custom policies. By default, the folder is empty. In the following example, we define a custom policy JuniorReader in the cap folder. 

  6. The AMS runtime plugin (@sap/ams) enforces your authorization policies to secure the application. Once the application is deployed to Cloud Foundry, you can manage the AMS policies in the Cloud Identity Service admin console. Find the deployed application under Applications tab and assign policies to users under Authorization Policies. 

Summary

For newly built applications, using Authorization Management Service (AMS) is generally recommended. The primary requirement for using AMS is that users must be replicated to the Identity Directory Service, which serves as the central hub for managing users in SAP BTP applications. This aligns with SAP's broader strategy to simplify and streamline user management across its platform in the future. 

More information

  • Configuring Authorization Policies
  • Assign Authorization Policies
  • CAP Developer Guide
  • Github Samples

entry.title: SAP Developer News, October 17th, 2024
entry.content:
ITEMS

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model September 2024 Release 

  • Details in Capire https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/sep24  

SAP Fiori Innovation Day 

  • SAP Fiori Innovation Day https://events.sap.com/eur/sap-fiori-innovation-day-heidelberg-2024/en/home 

SAP Cloud Integration News 

  • Message Status Overview in Cloud Integration https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/monitor-message-status-overview  
  • SMB Adapter in Cloud Integration https://api.sap.com/package/SMBAdapterforSAPIntegrationSuite/overview 
  • Message Transform utilities in Cloud Integration https://api.sap.com/package/com.sap.integration.cloud.utilities.MessageTransformations/overview 

Flexso Tech Day 2024 

  • Event website https://www.flexso.com/en/events/tech-day-2024  

Devtoberfest Week 4 

  • Devtoberfest sessio¡n calendar https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true 
  • Devtoberfest contest https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-contest/ba-p/13781593 

================================================================================ 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 
0:07 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model September 2024 Release 
2:28 SAP Fiori Innovation Day 
3:10 SAP Cloud Integration News 
4:26 Flexso Tech Day 2024 
5:22 Devtoberfest Week 4 

TRANSCRIPT

[Ajay] Hello, developers, September 2024 release of CAP is now available. The first update is about analytics for Capire Matomo is used to collect anonymized usage data for Capire, and users will have the option to disable the tracking. Coming to the Node.js updates, you can now import type definitions generated by the CDS typer on the top level code, even in the test code. Now we have richer JavaScript and TypeScript snippets in the compiler, which can now carry type information, allowing you to get a better insight into the APIs used there. Instance-based restrictions for bound actions and functions are now also enforced. SAP Cloud Application Event Hub is the new default offering for messaging in SAP Business Technology Platform, Integrating with SAP Cloud Application Event Hub is provided via a CDS plugin @cap-js/event-broker. Moving on to Java updates, we have enhancement to the code generator, where we now have stricter setters in the accessor interfaces. Accessor interfaces now offer the new factory method to access the data in the given map. Monitoring health status of MTX Sidecar is enabled with a new health indicator model provider. Parameter aliases in OData V4 for the entity key values and function parameter values are now also supported. You can easily add handler stubs using add handler CLI command in Java. A new serious property, ui5.version allows you to configure UI5 versions used by the Fiori preview. Finally, in the tools update, we have got beta release to visualize deployment descriptor mta.yaml file. This helps you to get a graphical overview of your microservices architecture and the interplay between the application and the services. This also works as a visual linter. There are two new experimental CDS lint checks, sql-null-comparison and no-java-keywords. For more details, please check the links provided in the description.

[Nico] Hi everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. The SAP Fiori Innovation Day will happen on November 19th in Heidelberg, Germany in the SAP App Haus, interestingly the same location we were at for SAP TechEd Virtual a couple of weeks ago. The SAP Fiori Innovation Day is open for everyone, and it will be a packed day with content about the SAP Fiori design system, SAP Fiori elements, AI of course, and it will also contain partner sessions. There will also be a hands-on session about the SAP Fiori elements flexible programming model. Check out the link in the description for more information and to register. See you. Bye.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. I have some exciting news to share with you today. The message status overview that we are so used to in SAP PI/PO is now available in SAP Integration Suite. The message status overview page provides a customizable overview of the messages process and their statuses in a selected time frame, as well as quick navigation to and from the message monitoring in your tenant. Also, there is a new adapter available to us in Cloud Integration. This is the SMB adapter, which will allow you to connect to a server using the SMB protocol, meaning that we can interact with an SMB server and read and write files. This is especially relevant for those on-premises integration scenarios. And finally, there is now an alternative solution in Cloud Integration to the MessageTransformBean adapter module that we often use in SAP PI/PO to aid with the transformation of the message during the runtime. It is in the form of Groovy scripts, which support most of the features that existed on the MTB module. Make sure to check it out. Ciao.

[DJ] If you're in or can get to the Brussels / Mechelen area of Belgium in early November, then there's a Tech Day happening on the latest SAP tools and technologies. Flexo Tech Day 2024 is happening on Thursday, the 7th of November. And following the welcome and keynote, There are sessions across three separate tracks, analytics and integration, extensions, and there's also a trending track. Now, the full agenda is online. The day is open to everyone, but spaces are limited. So head on over to the website to register. And at the end of the day, there's gonna be a beer tasting, mostly because the day is being held in a brewery. What's not to like? I'll be there. See you there too.

[Nico] Hi everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. We're just wrapping up week three of Devtoberfest, which means there's just one week left for Devtoberfest 2024. So next week we'll have five more days of packed content, all about SAP development. So on Monday, it will be ABAP and CAP again. On Tuesday, Tooling Tuesday, we'll talk about the SAP Build family. Wednesday is Integration Day. On Thursday we talk about MAD, which is machine learning, AI and data. And Friday will be Frontend Friday as well as Fun Friday. And for Fun Friday next week there will be Spooktoberfest, so expect a spooky day. Overall, next week I counted 24 sessions scheduled, that's a lot of content and it's great content too. We hope to see many more familiar faces as well as new faces. Welcome if you're new to this channel. Now's your chance to earn points in the Devtoberfest gameboard and to progress towards Nerdvana, which would qualify you for the Devtoberfest grand prize. So hope to see you around and see you next time.

entry.title: Announcing the New "In-Metro" Disaster Recovery Solution for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Overview

We’re excited to unveil the latest enhancement to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP):
The In-Metro Disaster Recovery (DR) solution.

This solution is designed to protect you against disasters affecting a single Availability Zone (AZ) with contractually committed SLAs RPO and RTO, offering enhanced resilience and uninterrupted operations for your cloud services.

 

What is "In-Metro" DR for SAP BTP?

The In-Metro DR solution is built to protect against local disasters by leveraging a Multi-AZ setup. It ensures that, in the event of a single AZ disaster, which is defined by SAP, services remain operational, data is protected, and business continues without significant downtime.

The key metrics are:

  • RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 5 minutes – Ensuring that data can be recovered with a maximum of 5 minutes' loss in case of a disaster.
  • RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 2 hours – Ensuring that the full system is restored and operational within 2 hours of an incident.

 

The Power of Multi-AZ Architecture

At the heart of In-Metro DR is the Multi-AZ (Multi Availability Zone) architecture, ensuring that your applications and databases are spread across different zones to safeguard against single-point failures.

This includes:

  • Active-active applications across 3 AZs: Applications are distributed across three AZs, with real-time data replication ensuring that if one AZ fails, the others automatically take over without impacting availability.
  • Active-passive databases across 2 AZs: Databases are set up in active-passive mode, with a failover mechanism in place to switch operations to a backup AZ if the primary one fails.

Through automatic failover, load balancing, and failure detection, the solution ensures that services stay online seamlessly. Internal stress tests, such as regular “Chaos Day” exercises, further reinforce the reliability of this DR solution.

 

Availability and Scope

 As of now, the In-Metro DR solution is not available for all SAP BTP services. While it already covers a wide range of services, its scope will continue to expand over time as we improve coverage and extend it to additional SAP BTP services. The services in scope will be regularly updated in the corresponding documents.

In-Metro DR is specifically designed for SAP-managed scenarios, meaning that customers using SAP BTP under SAP’s management can take advantage of the In-Metro DR setup without additional configuration or costs. However, for customer-managed scenarios, such as side-by-side extensions, the customer is responsible for configuring it accordingly (e.g. deploy the apps with multiple instances or configure data replicas on the persistency level).

 

 DR Planning and Regular Testing

 To ensure the highest levels of reliability:

  • The DR plan is updated every 12 months, ensuring that it incorporates the latest technology and best practices.
  • A DR test is conducted at least once a year to verify that the solution can meet its RPO and RTO commitments under real-world conditions.

The In-Metro DR solution currently supports SAP BTP regions hosted on both AWS and Azure cloud infrastructures, providing flexibility for your organization’s cloud setup.

 

 Next Steps

 By leveraging In-Metro DR within SAP-managed scenarios, you can trust that your critical data and services are protected with cutting-edge resilience, without the need for extra investment. For customer-managed scenarios, setting up corresponding HA and DR solutions will ensure your setup is equally resilient.

To explore which services are already covered and get all information, check the description in SAP Trust Center.

Alternatively, you can watch a deep dive into this new solution at SAP TechED: Watch the SAP TechED session on In-Metro DR.

entry.title: Configuring a destination for Action Editor
entry.content:
Motivation:

This blog focuses on the concept of destinations available for testing an Action within Action Project.

 

Parent Blog:

Actions Editor – Feature List, Usage and Maintenance of Actions Project | SAP Blogs

 

Concept & Need:

Actions can be executed by using destinations created in the subscriber's subaccount. Multiple destinations can be created in the cockpit, but not all of them are relevant for an Action Project, so filtering is necessary.

 

Destination Configuration:

In order to make a destination relevant for an Action Project, you should add a additional property "sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled" with a value “true” in the destination configuration. For projects created using a live API source, add another property "sap.build.usage" with the corresponding value from the table below to the destination configuration.

API SourceValue
Graph"GRAPH"
CAP"CAP"
RAP"RAP"
BTP"odata_gen"
Abhe"apihub_enterprise"
IFlow"integration_flow_rt"

Figure 1: Destination configuration

Note: For the projects created using API specification as source, all the destination with "sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled" property as true will be available.

 

Making a Destination available:

To enable a destination for testing an Action in Action Editor, the following process must be followed in control tower.

Navigate to control tower from lobby

Figure 2: Control tower button

Navigate to destinations inside control tower

Figure 3: Destinations button

Using the add functionality add the desired destination

Figure 4: Add destination button

The added destination will be available for selection in the destination dropdown present in test tab

Figure 5: Destination selection dropdown

 

Blog Reference:

    • Part 1 of the Blog Series: The New Actions Project – Major Changes including the Actions Editor Available Now !! | SAP Blogs
    • Part 2 of the Blog Series/Parent Blog: Actions Editor – Feature List, Usage and Maintenance of Actions Project

 

Thanks for reading and I hope it helped to understand the concept of object flattening within the Actions Editor. Please feel free to leave a comment if there are any questions and I would be happy to receive any feedback.

entry.title: You ask, we answer - Ask Me Anything Session (Oct 24)
entry.content:
During SAP TechEd 2024, SAP unveiled significant improvements to SAP Build, which included making SAP S/4HANA extension development more efficient, introducing new generative AI features, and providing broader access to ABAP development tools. These changes empower the developer community to innovate more easily and with increased agility. 

You can read all about the announcements in Michael Ameling's blog post: Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud! And the good news is: you can still access the session catalog to review the content and stay current with the latest innovations & enjoy Devtoberst sessions until October 25, check out the agenda here.

Now if you're curious about what you saw during the event and have some more questions, or you would like to dive deeper, here's a great opportunity:

We'll be hosting a live Ask Me Anything session on October 24, 3pm CET. You can watch it on YouTube, or click the preview above. In the session, the panel of experts will recap the features announced and answer your questions live. 

Can't join? No worries, drop your questions in the comments and the experts will answer them live for you to watch later 😉

Looking forward to seeing you there!

Cheers,
Maiara

entry.title: The next step of ABAPs evolution: ABAP Cloud now part of SAP Build
entry.content:
"We’ve always defined ourselves by the ability to overcome the impossible." (Movie: Interstellar, 2014)

Here we are again, with the next step in the evolution of ABAP.

ABAP Cloud

It’s been six years since SAP launched the so-called SAP Cloud Platform ABAP Environment, which allowed developers to build and run ABAP code in the cloud. Now called SAP BTP ABAP Environment, it is the SAP Platform-as-a-Service offering that enables developers to leverage their traditional ABAP skills to build and run ABAP applications in the cloud on SAP BTP, either as an extension to SAP S/4HANA (any edition) or as standalone applications. With the SAP BTP ABAP environment, we are targeting business application developers who have extensive SAP experience and domain knowledge, particularly around SAP ERP solutions. See Steampunk is going all-in and and It's Steampunk now, to read about it’s history.

Derived from this, something ingenious was introduced: The ABAP Cloud development model. ABAP Cloud is a perfect example of how innovation works. The story of ABAP Cloud began quite silent. In fact, ABAP Cloud was born in the SAP BTP ABAP environment, where  it’s the leading and only development model available for ABAP development. It’s the amazing result of the ABAP engineering team, which has evolved ABAP into ABAP Cloud, making it future-proof, upgrade-stable and, you could say, cloud native. 

With the ABAP Cloud development model, you can build cloud-ready and upgrade-stable business applications, services, and extensions. ABAP Cloud was introduced in the BTP ABAP environment, but like any other innovation success story, ABAP Cloud has expanded its home turf, it has grown in both scope and availability.

That’s how the idea was born to make ABAP Cloud available everywhere.

The result is that ABAP Cloud is already the underlying ABAP development model in SAP BTP ABAP environment and in S/4HANA Cloud, public edition as of 2208 (3 system landscape required). Additionally, it can be used in S/4HANA Cloud, private edition as well as in S/4HANA any premise from >2022. We highly recommend using ABAP Cloud already for your S/4HANA private cloud as well as S/4HANA on premise projects to set the guardrails to reach a clean core and preparing your cloud journey.

 

Picture 1: ABAP Cloud Availability

SAP Build 

Back to the opening statement: the next step in ABAP’s evolution. ABAP Cloud is now part of SAP Build! This is the amazing success story of how true integration works. How technology can overcome the borders of more than 40 years of evolutionary history. For more details on this great enhancement, read this technical blog post by Michael Ameling.

But what happens when the paths of SAP Build and ABAP Cloud cross to form the world’s strongest alliance for customer and partner development in the SAP ecosystem? What does this mean for you, our developer community?

With SAP Build, you can accelerate your ERP modernization, foster innovation and automate processes, all within a single comprehensive suite of extension development tools that includes low-code, pro-code and generative AI capabilities.

SAP Build provides you the flexibility to create and extend applications, automate processes and design digital workspaces with low-code, pro-code and generative AI tools. Its collaborative design allows diverse teams to build solutions faster using pre-built solutions, AI assistance, and seamless integration with SAP applications.​

It has never been easier from a developer’s perspective, since SAP Build provides you all the tools you need in one place,  so you can focus on the real development to be done. The SAP Build Lobby is the starting point for your Java/JavaScript projects and your ABAP Cloud projects.

Picture 2: SAP Build Lobby

Our goal is to provide you with a seamless development flow, regardless of the environment you’re working in most of the time. Examples of this integration include

  • The tool bridge between the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (ADT) and SAP Business Application Studio: The tool bridge connects ADT and BAS, which connects the front-end with the back-end development. It keeps the context of the services and entities as input for the Fiori generator.
  • The SAP Build Lobby as a single-entry point : You can start your ABAP development project from the lobby next to other build extension development options. You can access a list of ABAP systems directly from the lobby and keep a project reference to the ABAP system and development package.
  • The Graphical Modeler for ABAP: You can reuse CAP project models to generate ABAP Core Data Services (ABAP CDS) from it.   

In addition to all the benefits mentioned, it doesn’t matter if you’re building on-stack extensions in S/4HANA Cloud or side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP: SAP Build is the umbrella.

Picture 3: Extensibility Portfolio for S/4HANA public cloud edition

And this is only the beginning: We aim to integrate developer tools and capabilities into a seamless user centered offering and provide a clear developer tools strategy, so you can start building your SAP projects right away. Including ABAP Cloud in SAP Build is a testament to you as an ABAP Cloud developer. Your skills are more than important and strategic in the SAP application development and automation portfolio.

That is already exciting. And we haven’t even covered Joule’s capabilities in detail so far in this blog post.

We aim to increase the developer efficiency across the entire development lifecycle through generative AI and Joule embedded in all our IDEs and tools. Stay tuned for more news to come, especially on GenAI.

Resources

learn more about the topic:

  • Public Roadmap for SAP BTP, ABAP Environment
  • ABAP Cloud - Technical use cases and recommended technologies
  • Practicing Clean Core Extensibility For SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Certified Associate - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud
  • Extend SAP S/4HANA in the cloud and on premise with ABAP based extensions
  • ABAP Cloud FAQ
  • Building Low Code Extensions with Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA and SAP Build
  • Extend SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build
  • Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: What Certificate Type to Use for Custom Domains in the Neo Environment
entry.content:
By using the new add-domain-certificate console command, you can now add another type of certificate to the one you have for the SSL host. Before, you were able to bind only RSA certificates. However, now you can bind elliptic curve (ECC) certificates in addition to the RSA ones. See add-domain-certificate.

To adapt to the latest cryptography recommendations, you should use the ECC algorithm because it’s the most modern and secure cryptographic algorithm for server-side certificates.

 

 

Example: How to Set Up Your First ECC Certificate for the Custom Domain in the Neo Environment

1. Generate a certificate signing request (CSR):

neo generate-csr --account mysubaccount --user mymail@example.com --host hana.ondemand.com --name myECCcert --certificate-distinguished-name "C=BG,O=MyCompany,CN=www.mycompany.bg,E=admin@mycompany.bg" --elliptic-curve secp256r1

For more information, see generate-csr.

2. Send the CSR to a certificate authority (CA).

Copy and send the CSR to your trusted CA to sign the certificate.

3. Upload the SSL certificate you received from the CA:

neo upload-domain-certificate --account mysubaccount --user mymail@example.com --host hana.ondemand.com --name myECCcert --location ./certificate.pub

For more information, see upload-domain-certificate.

4. Bind the certificate:

neo bind-domain-certificate --account mysubaccount --user mymail@example.com --host hana.ondemand.com --ssl-host mysslhostname --certificate myECCcert

For more information, see bind-domain-certificate

 

Modern clients (browsers) support ECC. However, if you still have other clients that don’t support ECC, it’s possible to bind an additional uploaded RSA certificate next to the existing ECC one by using the add-domain-certificate console command. 

Example

neo add-domain-certificate --account mysubaccount --user mymail@example.com --host hana.ondemand.com --ssl-host mysslhostname --certificate myRSAcert

 

 

 

Furthermore, you can add an ECC certificate to an SSL host with a bound RSA certificate by using the add-domain-certificate console command.

Example

neo add-domain-certificate --account mysubaccount --user mymail@example.com --host hana.ondemand.com --ssl-host mysslhostname --certificate myECCcert

 

 

 

 

We’d appreciate your feedback on the usage of the ECC certificates.

entry.title: Shape the Future: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3, 2024!
entry.content:
We're excited to announce that the third cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2024 has begun, featuring 33 new projects designed to enhance innovation and collaboration for our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today's rapidly evolving business environment, staying ahead is crucial. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative offers you a unique chance to gain early insights into SAP's product developments. By participating, you'll collaborate directly with our developers and product managers, influencing the future direction of our products.

Act quickly! Registration for the projects is open until November 15, 2024. Don’t miss this chance to make your voice heard and shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

To assist you in exploring the projects, we’ve created a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the opportunities available:

SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Composable Enterprise: Reimagining Enterprise Software for Unprecedented Business Agility 
  • Help Where and When You Need It: User Assistance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Improve your Post Upgrade Tests(PUT) Experience - An Integrated and Smart approach to PUT
  • Insightful Analytics for Actual Costing
  • Joule applications to SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • Monitoring End to End Master Data Replications in SAP Cloud ALM
  • New Cost Settlement for real estate scenarios (Service Charge Settlement / CAM)
  • Purpose as base for processing of S/4HANA Business Partner personal data – concept and migration”
  • Research Project: Production Scheduling using AI
  • Research: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - AI: Field Mapping Proposal
  • Using AI to Extract Data from the Delivery Note to Update the Inbound Delivery

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI Agent Builder - A Low-Code Innovation with SAP Build
  • AI-Powered Autonomous Sandbox
  • Cloud Security: Improving the Encryption Key Management Experience
  • Discover SAP Build Process Automation Triggers: Empowering Experts to Automate and Extend Use Cases
  • GenAI Content Provenance and Authenticity via Watermarking
  • Investigation into a Customer Security Dashboard
  • Knowledge Graph RAG on unstructured Data

SAP Concur

  • Concur Expense - India GST handling
  • Concur Expense – Integrating Sales Opportunity Data with Concur Travel and Expense Data
  • Concur Expense - Preventing overspend through cost control, spend planning, and regulatory
  • Concur Expense - SAP Integration with Concur Solutions: Employee- and Benefit- focused Budget Management
  • Concur Expense – The Future of Expense Administration
  • Concur Travel - Trip Management / New Trip List
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Manage and track carbon consumption in T&E spend

Customer Experience

  • Redesigned SAP Sales Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Outlook

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP Globalization

  • AI-supported Extension of Electronic Document Processing of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Business AI
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research

Lifecycle Management

  • Business Service Management in SAP Cloud ALM operations
  • Clean Core / RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard in SAP Cloud ALM

Others

  • Early Talent Management with SAP SuccessFactors

We believe that collaboration is essential for driving innovation. By partnering with our customers and partners, we can collectively shape the future of SAP’s products and revolutionize business operations.

So, mark your calendars, rally your team, and let’s embark on this journey together. Together, we can foster progress, and transform industries.

Don’t wait—register now at influence.sap.com and join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3 in 2024!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: #05 - Understanding Project Methodology: The SAP Activate Methodology
entry.content:
The Importance of project management:

Effective project management is vital for delivering projects on time, within scope, and within budget. It provides structure and direction, helping teams navigate complex tasks and ensuring that everyone is aligned with the project's goals. Without a clear project management framework, projects can easily fall victim to scope creep, missed deadlines, and cost overruns.

Some common project management methodologies include:

  • Waterfall: A linear and sequential approach where each phase depends on the deliverables of the previous one.
  • Agile: An iterative approach that allows for flexibility and continuous improvement, often used in software development.
  • Scrum: A subset of Agile, focused on delivering smaller pieces of a project in "sprints" or time-boxed intervals.
  • Lean: A methodology that focuses on delivering maximum value to the customer by minimizing waste.

Each of these methodologies has its strengths, and the choice of methodology often depends on the nature of the project and the organization’s needs.

Introducing the SAP Activate Methodology:

At SAP, we’ve introduced a specialized project management methodology known as the SAP Activate Methodology. This methodology is designed specifically for SAP implementations and is tailored to meet the unique needs of businesses undergoing digital transformation.

What is the SAP Activate Methodology?

The SAP Activate Methodology is a simple, modular, and agile methodology used for implementing SAP solutions. It aims to accelerate implementations and drive continuous innovation by providing a standardized but flexible approach using best practices, agile principles, and a phased approach.

Key characteristics of the SAP Activate Methodology are:

  • Business Ownership: The business owns the solution, ensuring that the project aligns closely with business goals and delivers real value.
  • Lead with ‘Standard’ Best Practices: SAP Activate promotes using standard, pre-configured solutions that are based on SAP's best practices, minimizing the need for customization.
  • Agile, Modular, Scalable: The methodology supports an agile, scalable approach that can be adapted to different project sizes and complexities.
  • Rapid, Repeatable Delivery Steps: SAP Activate focuses on delivering value quickly and efficiently through repeatable steps.
  • Accelerators: Tools, templates, and content are provided to speed up the implementation process.
  • Quicker ROI: By using a fit-to-standard approach and best practices, organizations can achieve quicker return on investment.
  • SAP Solutions: designed to work with all SAP solutions, not just S/4HANA.
  • Six core project phases: Discover, Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy and Run.

Phases and main activities of the SAP Activate Methodology:

Access the SAP Activate Methodology tasks and accelerators via the SAP Roadmap Viewer:

The SAP Roadmap Viewer is a tool that provides access to SAP Activate Methodology implementation roadmaps. It offers a comprehensive view of project teams' activities, deliverables, and tasks associated with SAP implementations in in a structured way to support project planning and execution. It includes accelerator assets in document and hyperlink format to support implementation projects. The roadmaps are organized by logical categories or by SAP solution.

Recommendations for Success with the SAP Activate Methodology:

To fully leverage the SAP Activate Methodology, consider the following recommendations:

  • Learn about the SAP Activate Methodology: start with the Learning Journey Discovering SAP Activate Implementation Tools and Methodology to get a comprehensive understanding of how to manage your SAP projects using this methodology.

  • Join the SAP Activate Community: Engage with SAP Activate Methodology experts by joining the SAP Activate Community. This platform allows you to ask questions, share insights, and stay up-to-date with the latest news and developments in SAP Activate.

  • Explore the SAP Roadmap Viewer: The SAP Roadmap Viewer provides detailed guidance for your digital transformation journey. You can view and download SAP Activate Methodology assets and accelerators and even provide feedback to SAP.

  • Adapt to your project situation: While the SAP Activate Methodology provides a robust framework, it’s important to adapt the methodology to your specific project situation. Trust the process, but be flexible enough to make adjustments as needed to ensure the best outcomes.

Conclusion:

Understanding and implementing the right project methodology is crucial to the success of your SAP project. The SAP Activate Methodology offers a powerful, business-driven approach that aligns closely with best practices and the needs of modern businesses. By following the SAP Activate Methodology, engaging with the community, and utilizing available resources, you can ensure that your project is not only successful but also delivers rapid and sustainable value to your organization.
